name: South Carolina
tagline: A $9B Google data-center buildout, a citizen-facing AI assistant going live, and a CIO running a 'protect, promote, pursue' AI playbook across 80+ agencies — South Carolina is modernizing fast on a centralized buying model.

execSummary.summary: South Carolina budgets annually (fiscal year July–June) and runs a centralized technology model: the Division of Technology Operations (DTO) inside the Department of Administration delivers enterprise IT, while the Division of Procurement Services / Materials Management Office owns statewide contracts. Three things matter to a Dell seller right now. First, an historic AI-infrastructure buildout is landing — Google committed roughly $9B through 2027 across Berkeley and Dorchester counties, and Meta is building an ~$800M AI-optimized data center in Aiken County. Second, the state is operationalizing AI under CIO Nathan Hogue's 'three Ps' framework (protect, promote, pursue), an AI Center of Excellence that vets proposals, and the citizen-facing 'Bradley' AI assistant (with Tyler Technologies). Third, centralized cybersecurity spans 80+ agencies, with a Deloitte-run security operations center, next-gen firewalls, and legacy modernization opening the door to cloud and AI. The buying motion runs through SC statewide term contracts; greenfield data centers and agency modernization are the strongest Dell tailwinds.
opportunities: AI-infrastructure demand tied to the Google ($9B) and Meta (~$800M) data-center buildout — PowerEdge, networking, and services.
Legacy application and infrastructure modernization across 80+ agencies as the state unlocks 'trapped' data for cloud and AI.
Governed, on-prem / private-cloud AI for sensitive citizen data as agencies move proposals through the AI Center of Excellence.
Endpoint, client, and lifecycle refresh across a centralized enterprise-IT estate run by DTO.

budget.intro: South Carolina enacts an annual budget on a July–June fiscal year. The FY2025-26 General Appropriations Act (H.4025) totals roughly $41.6B in all funds, of which about $13.2B is general fund. There is no single published 'state IT budget' line — technology spend is distributed across agencies, with enterprise services centralized in the Division of Technology Operations and statewide buying run through Procurement Services. Governor McMaster's FY2026-27 executive budget (announced January 2026) totals ~$42.8B all funds / ~$14.0B general fund and is now moving through the General Assembly.
notes: South Carolina budgets annually, not biennially — confirm the current enacted fiscal year before forecasting.
There is no single 'state IT budget' line item; technology dollars sit inside agency appropriations and enterprise services run by DTO.

FY2027 (governor's proposed, per NASBO): $14.0B general fund, +6.0% vs FY2026; $42.8B all funds. Sets aside $1.4B (10% of FY2025 GF revenue) across the General and Capital Reserve Funds and proposes a new Office of Statewide Data led by a Chief Data Officer.

initiatives.name: Data-Center Buildout (Google + Meta)
initiatives.summary: Google committed roughly $9B through 2027 to expand its Berkeley County campus and complete two new campuses in Dorchester County, and Meta is building an ~$800M, ~715,000-sq-ft AI-optimized data center in Aiken County. This is the strongest tailwind for Dell AI-infrastructure and ISG demand in the state, with adjacent ecosystem and supplier opportunity.

initiatives.name: Statewide AI Program & Center of Excellence
initiatives.summary: CIO Nathan Hogue's 'three Ps' framework (protect, promote, pursue) anchors the state's AI strategy. An AI Center of Excellence — convening agencies, higher ed, and industry — held its first meeting in February 2025 and vets proposals on privacy and security before rollout. An opening for governed, auditable, private/on-prem AI on sensitive citizen data.

initiatives.name: Bradley — Citizen AI Assistant
initiatives.summary: Built with Tyler Technologies, 'Bradley' lets citizens use plain language to apply to be a notary, pay taxes, or find water-bill information, with identity management and fraud detection. It signals appetite for AI-driven service delivery and the compute, data, and security backbone such workloads require.

initiatives.name: Enterprise Cybersecurity & Modernization
initiatives.summary: South Carolina centrally monitors 80+ agencies, runs a Deloitte-operated security operations center, upgraded next-gen firewall services, and hardened Office 365. Hogue frames legacy modernization as foundational to unlocking trapped data for cloud and AI — a multi-year infrastructure and services runway.

procurement.intro: South Carolina runs a centralized procurement model under the Consolidated Procurement Code. The Division of Procurement Services (within the Department of Administration) is the state's central procurement authority; its Materials Management Office and an Information Technology Management Office establish open-ended statewide term contracts that leverage the collective buying power of state agencies and political subdivisions. Enterprise IT delivery is centralized in the Division of Technology Operations (DTO).

vehicles.name: Statewide Term Contracts
vehicles.detail: Primary vehicle. Pre-competed, open-ended contracts open to state agencies and political subdivisions; searchable via procurement.sc.gov. Confirm Dell agreements and resellers are current and correctly scoped.

vehicles.name: IT Management Office
vehicles.detail: Information Technology Management Officer within Procurement Services leads IT-specific acquisitions and large technology procurements.

vehicles.name: DTO Enterprise Services
vehicles.detail: Division of Technology Operations delivers centralized enterprise IT, security monitoring, and shared services for participating agencies.

entities.name: Division of Procurement Services / MMO
entities.role: Central procurement authority; owns statewide term contracts and IT acquisitions.

entities.name: Division of Technology Operations (DTO)
entities.role: Enterprise IT, cybersecurity monitoring (80+ agencies), and shared services.

entities.name: Department of Administration
entities.role: Houses DTO and Procurement Services; sets statewide technology and budget direction.

entities.name: Clemson University & University of South Carolina
entities.role: Large independent research-computing and AI-infrastructure buyers.

procurement.competition: South Carolina is a centralized, contract-driven market — differentiation comes from statewide-contract coverage, AI-infrastructure capability tied to the data-center boom, and services that map to enterprise modernization and the AI Center of Excellence rather than price alone. Incumbents like Deloitte (SOC) and Tyler Technologies (citizen services) hold meaningful agency relationships.

powerStructure.name: Henry McMaster
powerStructure.title: Governor
powerStructure.note: Sets the executive budget; champions the data-center and economic-development agenda. Term-limited — leaves office end of 2026; endorsed Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette to succeed him. Expect new administration and appointees in early 2027.

powerStructure.name: Nathan Hogue
powerStructure.title: State CIO
powerStructure.note: Owns statewide tech direction; author of the 'three Ps' AI framework and AI Center of Excellence. Career SC IT leader since 2001.

powerStructure.name: Division of Procurement Services
powerStructure.title: Central procurement / statewide contracts
powerStructure.note: Materials Management Office + IT Management Office own the statewide buying motion.

powerStructure.name: House AI & Cybersecurity Committee
powerStructure.title: Legislative oversight
powerStructure.note: Regulations, Administrative Procedures, AI & Cybersecurity Committee — tracks AI/cyber policy.

news.title: FY2026-27 budget still in conference ahead of June 30 deadline
news.impact: House (~$15.4B GF) and Senate (~$15.3B GF, passed April 23) versions unresolved in conference committee as of late June; lawmakers due back June 25. No signed appropriations act yet — confirm enactment before forecasting.

news.title: Statewide AI report (Edition 0.1) published via SC Research Authority
news.impact: Signals a maturing, governed AI agenda across state government.

news.title: McMaster announces FY2026-27 executive budget (~$42.8B all funds)
news.impact: Sets spending priorities; budget moves through the General Assembly for July start.

news.title: Gov. McMaster proposed a $14.0B general-fund FY2027 budget
news.impact: $42.8B all funds, +9.2% vs FY2026; sets aside $1.4B across the General and Capital Reserve Funds. Proposes a new Office of Statewide Data led by a Chief Data Officer to improve data management and use across agencies.

news.title: Google confirms ~$9B SC data-center expansion through 2027
news.impact: Largest AI-infrastructure tailwind in the state; Berkeley + Dorchester campuses.

news.title: Meta announces ~$800M AI-optimized data center in Aiken County
news.impact: First Meta operation in SC; reinforces the state as an AI-infrastructure hub.

news.title: 'Bradley' citizen AI assistant goes live (with Tyler Technologies)
news.impact: AI-driven service delivery raises demand for compute, data, and security backbone.

dellActions: Lead with AI-infrastructure: tie Dell ISG / AI Factory offerings to the Google and Meta data-center buildout and the Clemson/USC research-computing buyers.
Pursue enterprise modernization across DTO's 80+-agency estate — position infrastructure and services that unlock legacy data for cloud and AI.
Position governed, private/on-prem AI for sensitive citizen workloads as proposals move through the AI Center of Excellence.
Validate Dell's statewide term contract coverage and resellers via Procurement Services / the IT Management Office for the agencies you're targeting.
Engage on the cybersecurity stack (SOC, next-gen firewall, endpoint) where Deloitte and incumbents operate — find the secure-compute and infrastructure gaps.
